title: Map Command, Page State, Navigation Invariants, and Transactional Updates · Guided Systems Studio
summary: A regional field-research team incrementally builds transactional Pokemon page navigator from original fixed PokéAPI fixtures while retaining prior Go, HTTP, Git, quality-gate, accessibility, security, testing, cancellation, and recovery requirements. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of transactional Pokemon page navigator while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence de…
objectives: Store current resource, offset, limit, next, previous, result identities, and revision as one explicit page state.
Compute candidate navigation, fetch and validate it, then commit state only after success.
Disable or explain next and back at absent-link boundaries and reject negative, excessive, or mismatched pagination state.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Pokedex product engineering and Go Commit to the mission model · Workshop Pokedex Go Page State Mod…
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop Go team handles case 9450 during a fair-use API stewardship review. Build deterministic Go evidence to commit only admitted page transitions while preserving cursor state on failure; failed and canceled commands must preserve the prior admitted state; then change one catch, owned record, encounter relation, or history state and prove the prior invariant. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original fixtures only. Real PokéAPI traffic, DNS, sockets, TLS, host files, terminals, signals, race and leak claims, load, cross-platform packaging, accessibility review, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Store current resource, offset, limit, next, previous, result identities, and revision as one explicit page state.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Pokedex product engineering and Go Commit to the mission model · Workshop Pokedex Go Page State Mod….
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for store current resource, offset, limit, next, previous, result identities, and revision as one explicit page state. For Pokedex product engineering and Go Commit to the mission model · Workshop Pokedex Go Page State Mod…, keep Pokedex Go Page State Model connected to executable Go behavior evidence.

steps.title: Pokedex product engineering and Go Extend the cumulative artifact · Workshop Pokedex Go Map Transit…
steps.instruction:
Append a compile-ready pure-Go function headed "// Evidence: pokedex-go-map-transition-tests-1u1hv8" that uses case 2468 to commit only admitted page transitions while preserving cursor state on failure. Keep defaults runnable and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not open sockets, resolve DNS, contact PokéAPI, read or write host files, handle real terminal or OS signals, spawn unbounded work, or claim race, leak, load, accessibility, cross-platform, package, or production behavior; verify those boundaries later with Go 1.26.5, original fixtures, controlled RoundTrippers and httptest servers, race and fuzz runs, temporary files, subprocess and signal harnesses, inspected artifacts, representative platforms, accessibility review, capacity, rollback, recovery, and release gates.
